skill.name: api-skill
skill.description: Provides a minimal API-focused prompt context for AI coding assistants.
skill.summary: A lightweight skill for API-related prompting.
skill.author: 0xSero

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 2 is a false positive. The flagged line is only front matter description text, and no code, prompt injection, network behavior, or secret handling was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 2, but that line is YAML description text: "description: Updated API skill". No cryptographic algorithm or executable code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is a plain metadata description line. There is no crypto API, hash algorithm, command, or script in the file.

content.value_statement: API work often needs concise direction before implementation starts. This skill supplies a minimal API-focused prompt context for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Provides a short API-focused prompt context.
Identifies the skill as api-skill through front matter.
Uses automatic model selection through its metadata.
Can guide an assistant toward API-related coding tasks.
Works as a lightweight prompt-only skill without scripts.
limitations: Does not include API design templates or checklists.
Does not contain runnable code, tests, or automation.
Does not specify a framework, language, or API style.
Requires the user to provide task details and constraints.

use_cases.title: Draft API Endpoints
use_cases.description: Use the skill to frame requests for endpoint design, route naming, and response structure.

use_cases.title: Review API Requirements
use_cases.description: Use the skill to keep an assistant focused on API behavior, inputs, outputs, and edge cases.

use_cases.title: Prepare Integration Prompts
use_cases.description: Use the skill before asking for integration steps between an application and an external API.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use api-skill to describe the endpoint I need, including request fields, response fields, and error cases.

prompt_templates.title: Plan API Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use api-skill to plan changes for this API feature. Include affected endpoints, compatibility risks, and test cases.

prompt_templates.title: Review an API Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Use api-skill to review this API contract for missing fields, unclear errors, and client integration risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Integration Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use api-skill to design an integration strategy for this external API. Include authentication, retries, rate limits, and observability.

output_examples.input: Plan a user profile endpoint for a web application.
output_examples.output: A concise endpoint plan with method, path, request fields, response fields, validation rules, and expected errors.

output_examples.input: Review an API change for backward compatibility.
output: A list of fields or behaviors that may break clients.
Suggested tests for old and new client behavior.

best_practices: Provide the API goal, expected clients, and data model before asking for output.
Ask for request, response, error, and validation details in one prompt.
Review generated API guidance against your existing standards and security requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not expect the skill to infer a full API specification without context.
Do not use it as a replacement for security review or threat modeling.
Do not assume it validates implementation code or production behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides a minimal prompt context for API-related work with supported AI coding assistants.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains only a short SKILL.md prompt and metadata.

faq.question: Can it design complete API specifications?
faq.answer: It can help frame API design prompts, but users must provide requirements and review the output.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it access files or networks?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed skill has no scripts, commands, or network calls.

faq.question: Was the static security finding confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The weak cryptography alert was a false positive on a description metadata line.
